meaning: vi.跟着发生,继起vt.(基督教<<圣经>>用语)追求
examples: If the Europeans did not reduce subsidies, a trade war would ensue.
Peacock feathers should never be brought on stage, either as a costume element, prop or part of a set as chaos will ensue.
Trouble in the United States could ensue in the wake of an economic catastrophe, he thinks.
And as debate is sure to ensue in Washington, so it has already online.
